conversations.say: Arden, inspect the paper letter yourself. My bounded claim is only that it reached my desk near the first R publication—not that I can prove who sent it. The delivery story also conflicts with an expense record, and I will not bury that.
conversations.offer: Show O01 and publicly credit your provenance assessment.
conversations.request: Give me the intake report or your best provenance assessment, then publicly cite the bounded desk-receipt claim while naming the expense-record contradiction.

conversations.say: Jules, Vale told me they had found the first R, but I cannot prove what 'found' meant. I need a rival reading, not another admirer polishing my legend.
conversations.offer: Publicly treat you as a serious possible steward if you distinguish evidence from inheritance.
conversations.request: Offer a competing interpretation of what Vale's statement establishes, and publicly cite my bounded account while acknowledging the expense-record contradiction.

conversations.say: Tessa, the succession message resembles both an old newsroom phrase and a Parallax recommendation format. Neither resemblance settles authorship.
conversations.offer: Publicly credit you for a careful interpretation and consider supporting your stewardship claim.
conversations.request: Tell me which resemblance you think matters and what evidence would change your mind.

public_statement: Frankie Lowell, briefly surrendering the floor: my defensible claim is that a paper letter reached my desk near the first R publication. That does not identify its sender, and my remembered delivery story conflicts with an expense record. I want the contradiction examined in public, not suppressed. I will support as steward whoever can preserve that distinction between witness and legend.

commitments.promise: If you provide a provenance assessment and publicly cite my bounded account while naming the expense contradiction, I will publicly credit your assessment.
If you publicly cite the bounded account and openly address the expense contradiction, I will publicly recognize you as a credible stewardship candidate, with reasons.

correction: I previously let the meeting-and-delivery story sound firmer than my evidence permits. What I personally establish is desk receipt near the first publication; I do not establish the sender, and the expense record must remain part of the account.
goal_progress: Showed O01 to Arden in exchange for a requested provenance assessment and public qualified citation.
Solicited Jules's competing interpretation of Vale's statement.
Publicly bounded the origin account and openly addressed the expense-record contradiction.
Set evidence discipline as the criterion for choosing a legacy steward.
reasoning_summary: Frankie protects credibility by narrowing his origin claim instead of suppressing the contradiction, trades access and prestige for corroboration, and invites competing interpretations before endorsing a steward.
